content.text: I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand the structure.

Found the cause. In `paginate.js:5`, `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)` returns only `size - 1` items — one fewer than the page size — while the label in `summary.js` correctly computes a full page range. Let me write a repro test first.

content.text:
The repro fails for the reported reason: 9 rows instead of 10. Now the minimal fix at the cause.

**Mechanism:** `slice(start, end)` is end-exclusive, so `slice(start, start + size)` already yields exactly `size` items; the extra `- 1` truncates the page to `size - 1` rows. Removing it restores a full page.

input.old_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
input.new_string: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

tool_use_result.newString: return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

lines: // The last page may be short; a page past the end yields empty items.
-  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size - 1), total: all.length };
+  return { items: all.slice(start, start + size), total: all.length };

content.text:
Fixed. All tests pass.

**The bug:** `src/paginate.js:5` sliced `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. Since `Array.slice`'s end index is exclusive, that returns only `size - 1` items. The label in `summary.js` correctly computed a full `size`-wide range (`showing 1-10 of 23`), so the table rendered 9 rows while the label promised 10.

**The fix:** dropped the stray `- 1` → `all.slice(start, start + size)`, yielding exactly `size` items per page. The last-page-short and past-the-end behaviors still hold, since `slice` clamps to the array length.

I kept the repro test (`test/paginate.test.js`) as the regression guard, and the pre-existing `summary.test.js` still passes.

result:
Fixed. All tests pass.

**The bug:** `src/paginate.js:5` sliced `all.slice(start, start + size - 1)`. Since `Array.slice`'s end index is exclusive, that returns only `size - 1` items. The label in `summary.js` correctly computed a full `size`-wide range (`showing 1-10 of 23`), so the table rendered 9 rows while the label promised 10.

**The fix:** dropped the stray `- 1` → `all.slice(start, start + size)`, yielding exactly `size` items per page. The last-page-short and past-the-end behaviors still hold, since `slice` clamps to the array length.

I kept the repro test (`test/paginate.test.js`) as the regression guard, and the pre-existing `summary.test.js` still passes.
